steps.title: Substitute the expression for t
steps.content:
Start with $p^{\frac{4}{5}} = t^{\frac{6}{5}}$ and the relation $t = p^{2n-1}$. Substitute the expression for $t$:
$$p^{\frac{4}{5}} = (p^{2n-1})^{\frac{6}{5}}$$

steps.title: Apply power rule and equate exponents
steps.content:
Simplify the right side using the power rule $(a^m)^k = a^{mk}$:
$$p^{\frac{4}{5}} = p^{\frac{6(2n-1)}{5}}$$ Since the bases are equal and $p \gt  1$, the exponents must be equal:
$$\frac{4}{5} = \frac{6(2n-1)}{5}$$

steps.title: Solve for n
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by 5:
$$4 = 6(2n-1)$$ Distribute:
$$4 = 12n - 6$$ Add 6 to both sides:
$$10 = 12n$$ Divide by 12:
$$n = \frac{10}{12} = \frac{5}{6}$$
